name: FocusForge — Anti-Distraction Shield
description: Blocks distracting websites during Focus Sessions. Powered by FocusForge.
_comment_service_worker: The background service worker handles all URL interception logic. It runs persistently by using chrome.alarms to keep itself alive (MV3 service workers can be killed after ~30s of inactivity).
_comment_permissions: declarativeNetRequest — Intercept/redirect/block network requests at the browser engine level. This is INSTANTANEOUS (no script roundtrip) and the only MV3-approved way to block requests.
storage — Persist the extension token, cached session status, and blocklist in chrome.storage.local.
alarms — Schedule periodic polling to keep the service worker alive and refresh session state.
tabs — Read the active tab URL so we can pass context (site name) to the Roast API.
notifications — Show a native OS notification when a block fires.
host_permissions — Required by declarativeNetRequest to redirect/block specific domains.
_comment_host_permissions: Broad match is required to allow declarativeNetRequest rules to fire on any domain. In a production extension, you'd enumerate specific domains for tighter security.
_comment_action: The popup HTML file — shown when the user clicks the extension icon in the toolbar.
_comment_content_scripts: Content scripts are injected into matching pages after the DOM is ready.
IMPORTANT: MV3 prohibits inline scripts (<script> tags in injected HTML).
All DOM manipulation goes in content.js; styles in content.css.
The content script provides a secondary visual overlay as a UX reinforcement
AFTER declarativeNetRequest has already fired the redirect.
_comment_web_accessible_resources: The blocked.html page and its assets need to be accessible as extension pages referenced by redirect rules.
_comment_content_security_policy: MV3 CSP is strict by default. We explicitly define it to document the surface area.
NO eval(), NO inline scripts. All scripts must be loaded from extension files.
connect-src allows calls to our Next.js web-app API and to ourselves (chrome-extension://).

content_security_policy.extension_pages: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; connect-src 'self' https://focus-forge.vercel.app https://localhost:3000;
